\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%

\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Parameters
df <- 8  # Degrees of freedom
x <- seq(0, 20, by = 0.1)  # Range of x values
tail_start <- 15.51  # Starting point for the shaded tail

# Create a data frame for the chi-square distribution
data <- data.frame(
  x = x,
  y = dchisq(x, df = df)
)

# Create the plot
chisq_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_line(color = "#00338D", size = 1) +
  geom_area(
    data = subset(data, x >= tail_start),
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    fill = "#00338D",
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  labs(x="x", y="density")

chisq_plot
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}


\noindent Using the built-in test
